G","placeholder":"","source":"https://content.protocols.io/bimk4kcn.jpg","width":66}},"6":{"type":"image","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"615CD06A341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":74,"is_image":true,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":null,\"entityMap\":null}","mime":"image/jpeg","original_name":"WH1.JPG","placeholder":"","source":"https://content.protocols.io/bimm4kcn.jpg","width":105}}}}
steps: ¼ - ½ of the pancreas is placed into 5 ml Acid-Ethanol (1.5% HCl in 70% EtOH) in a 15 ml conical vial.
Homogenize tissue (I use a Polytron homogenizer).
Centrifuge at 2000 rpm 15 min at 4ºC (Sorvall RT6000).
Transfer aqueous solution to a new 15 ml conical vial.
Neutralize 100 µl of Acid-Ethanol extract with 100 µl 1M Tris pH 7.5.
Dilute further (1:100, 1:1000, or 1:5000 depending upon the strain) in Insulin ELISA sample diluent.
Run diluted sample on Insulin ELISA (Exocell). Calculate ng/ml with appropriate dilution factor.
Run 20 µl of the neutralized solution in a Bradford Assay (250 µl Coomassie Blue Reagent, Thermo Scientific) against a standard curve. Calculate µg/ml with appropriate dilution factor.
Divide Insulin content ng/ml by Protein content µg/ml.
creator: Lili Liang
